phd_education_lcid.major_name: Education (PhD) — Learning, Cognition, Instruction, and Development (LCID)
phd_education_lcid.description: The LCID concentration prepares researchers focused on classroom learning, teaching, and cognition. Students choose a specialization from: early childhood education, educational psychology, human development, language education, learning sciences, literacy education, mathematics education, science education, social studies education, special education, or statistics and measurement.

required_research_methods.description: Two research methods courses — one qualitative and one quantitative (6 credits)

concentration_coursework.description: Specialization coursework determined in consultation with advisor (minimum 36 credits from LCID-area courses)
notes: Learning Sciences specialization requires: EDPD582 (Cognition and Instruction) and DSLE610 (Design-Based Research)
Statistics and Measurement specialization requires: ESME531, ESME532, EDPD687, EDPD683
Early Childhood requires: LCD512, LCD520, LCD521, LCD522
Literacy Education requires advanced reading/language education seminars
Mathematics Education requires: MAED541, MAED542, EDPD661
Science Education requires: SCED650, SCED651
Special Education requires: SPED605, SPED615, SPED630
Educational Psychology requires: EDPD581, EDPD582, EDPY605

milestones.qualifying_examination: Must be passed before formal doctoral candidacy; typically completed after core coursework
milestones.dissertation_proposal: Approved proposal required before dissertation research
milestones.dissertation_defense: Final oral defense of original research dissertation
milestones.teaching_portfolio: Required: statement of teaching philosophy, student evaluations, faculty lesson observations, and instructional artifacts

constraints: Minimum 72 credits: at least 48 coursework + 24 dissertation research.
Minimum GPA of 3.25 required throughout the program.
At least three years of full-time study beyond the bachelor's degree.
Two proseminars (EDPD501, EDPD503) required of all PhD students.
One qualitative and one quantitative research methods course required.
Qualifying exam must be passed before advancing to candidacy.
Dissertation committee: advisor + at least two other faculty members.
notes: LCID concentrations include specializations in 11 areas; program plan designed with faculty advisor.
Source: catalogs.rutgers.edu/generated/gse_2425/pg112.html

phd_education_top.description: The TOP concentration prepares scholars for research in higher education policy, organizational leadership, and international/comparative education settings. Students work in governmental, non-governmental, and policy research organizations.

required_research_methods.description: Two research methods courses — one qualitative and one quantitative (6 credits)

concentration_coursework.description: Policy, organization, and higher education coursework in consultation with advisor (minimum 36 credits)

milestones.qualifying_examination: Must be passed before formal doctoral candidacy
milestones.dissertation_proposal: Approved proposal required before dissertation research
milestones.dissertation_defense: Final oral defense of original research dissertation
milestones.teaching_portfolio: Required: statement of teaching philosophy, student evaluations, faculty lesson observations, and instructional artifacts

constraints: Minimum 72 credits: at least 48 coursework + 24 dissertation research.
Minimum GPA of 3.25 required.
At least three years of full-time study beyond the bachelor's degree.
Two proseminars (EDPD501, EDPD503) required.
One qualitative and one quantitative research methods course required.
Dissertation committee: advisor + at least two other faculty members.
notes: TOP prepares scholars for academic and policy research careers in higher education and policy organizations.
Source: catalogs.rutgers.edu/generated/gse_2425/pg112.html

phd_higher_education.description: Interdisciplinary program preparing scholars for research, policy, and leadership in higher education institutions, foundations, and governmental organizations. Offered through a collaboration of the GSE and the Graduate School–New Brunswick.

required_research_methods.description: Research methods core — qualitative and quantitative (15 credits)

additional_methods.description: Three additional methods courses selected from approved list (9 credits)

electives.description: Higher education elective courses in consultation with advisor (15 credits)

milestones.qualifying_examination: Comprehensive examination before advancing to candidacy
milestones.dissertation_proposal: Proposal defense required before dissertation research

constraints: Minimum 72 credits: 48 coursework + 24 dissertation.
Minimum GPA of 3.25 required.
Interdisciplinary program; students may take relevant courses in affiliated departments.
Program director approval required for independent study credits.
notes: Program has been offered since 1997; admission is highly competitive.
Director: Ebelia Hernandez (848-932-0844).
Source: catalogs.rutgers.edu/generated/gse_2425/pg113.html; gse.rutgers.edu/degree/ph-d-in-higher-education-program/

edd_educational_leadership.description: Executive-format EdD designed for practicing educational leaders (PK-12 or higher education). Cohort-based program delivered over three years and one summer. Prepares leaders to use evidence-based inquiry to improve educational organizations. Optional principal and school administrator certification track available.

core_courses.description: EdD core courses shared by all students (24 credits)

certification_option.description: For NJ principal/school administrator certification, substitute these courses (contact program for details)
notes: EADM530 Internship in Educational Administration I
EADM531 Internship in Educational Administration II
EADM507 Education Law or EADM606 Higher Education Law

dissertation.notes: Applied dissertation addressing a real problem of practice in the student's professional context.

milestones.qualifying_review: End of Year 1 satisfactory progress review
milestones.dissertation_proposal: Dissertation committee and approved proposal required by Year 2

constraints: Total: 72 credits (24 core + 24 concentration + 24 dissertation).
Cohort-based; students must enter and progress with their cohort.
Executive format: classes typically held on weekends or evenings.
Students must hold a master's degree and significant professional experience.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required for good academic standing.
Program has been offered since 1930.
notes: Two certification sub-tracks: standard Educational Leadership, or Educational Leadership + NJ Principal and School Administrator Certification.
Source: gse.rutgers.edu/edd-program/; catalogs.rutgers.edu/generated/gse_2425/pg129.html

edd_education_culture_society.description: Prepares scholar-practitioners for leadership in educational and community organizations through lenses of culture, social justice, and equity. Cohort-based executive format.

concentration_courses.description: Education, Culture, and Society concentration courses (24 credits)

milestones.qualifying_review: End of Year 1 satisfactory progress review
milestones.dissertation_proposal: Approved by dissertation committee in Year 2

constraints: Total: 72 credits (24 core + 24 concentration + 24 dissertation).
Cohort-based executive format.
Students must hold a master's degree and professional experience.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
notes: Source: gse.rutgers.edu/degree/ed-d-program-education-culture-and-society-concentration/; catalogs.rutgers.edu/generated/gse_2425/pg130.html

edd_design_learning_environments.description: Prepares practitioners and scholars to design innovative learning environments using research-based methods. Focuses on technology-enhanced learning, informal learning, and instructional design. Cohort-based.

concentration_courses.description: Design of Learning Environments concentration courses (24 credits)

constraints: Total: 72 credits (24 core + 24 concentration + 24 dissertation).
Cohort-based; students enter as a cohort and progress together.
Students must hold a master's degree.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
notes: Source: gse.rutgers.edu/degree/ed-d-program-design-of-learning-environments-concentration/; catalogs.rutgers.edu/generated/gse_2425/pg131.html

edd_special_education.description: Prepares leaders in special education for roles in schools, districts, and agencies. Focuses on evidence-based intervention design, inclusive practices, and educational policy affecting individuals with disabilities. Cohort-based.

electives.description: 12 credits of electives from special education or related fields

constraints: Total: 72 credits (24 core + 24 concentration + 24 dissertation).
Cohort-based executive format.
Students must hold a master's degree and professional experience in special education or related field.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
notes: Source: gse.rutgers.edu/degree/ed-d-program-special-education-concentration/; catalogs.rutgers.edu/generated/gse_2425/pg132.html

edd_teacher_leadership.description: Designed for accomplished classroom teachers who want to become instructional leaders and catalysts for school improvement. Executive cohort format. Graduates earn an EdD and endorsement as Teacher Leader.
constraints: Total: 72 credits (24 core + 24 concentration + 24 dissertation).
Cohort-based; students must be practicing K–12 teachers.
Students must hold a master's degree.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
notes: Graduates may also be eligible to apply for the NJ Teacher Leader endorsement.
Source: gse.rutgers.edu/degree/ed-d-program-teacher-leadership/; catalogs.rutgers.edu/generated/gse_2425/pg133.html

edm_college_student_affairs_ft.description: Prepares professionals for student affairs administration in colleges and universities. Two-year full-time program with supervised field experience. Students gain skills in student development, assessment, program design, diversity, and leadership.

field_experience.notes: Year-long placement in a college or university student affairs office.

electives.description: Elective courses chosen in consultation with advisor (9 credits)

constraints: Total: 48 credits (21 core + 18 field experience + 9 electives).
Field placements are competitive; arranged through the GSE Office of Field Experiences.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
Full-time enrollment required for first year.
notes: Prepares graduates for roles including residence life, advising, multicultural affairs, and student activities.
Source: gse.rutgers.edu/degree/ed-m-in-college-student-affairs/; catalogs.rutgers.edu/generated/gse_2425/pg136.html

edm_college_student_affairs_exec.major_name: College Student Affairs (EdM) — Part-Time Executive Management Track
edm_college_student_affairs_exec.description: Part-time executive track for working student affairs and higher education professionals. Coursework delivered in an accelerated hybrid format.

electives.description: Electives chosen in consultation with advisor; at least 6 credits from Special Topics in CSA (9 credits)

constraints: Total: 30 credits.
Designed for working professionals; part-time enrollment.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
At least 6 of the 9 elective credits must come from Special Topics in CSA (CSA640-645).
notes: Source: gse.rutgers.edu/degree/ed-m-in-college-student-affairs/; catalogs.rutgers.edu/generated/gse_2425/pg137.html

edm_educational_admin_option_c.major_name: Educational Administration and Supervision (EdM) — Option C (No Certification)
edm_educational_admin_option_c.description: Non-certification track for students seeking graduate study in educational administration without pursuing NJ principal or supervisor certification. Flexible program plan developed with advisor.

electives.description: Elective courses from Educational Administration or related GSE departments (18 credits)

constraints: Total: 30 credits minimum.
No principal or supervisor internship required (Option A and B include internship/certification).
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
Program plan developed with faculty advisor.
notes: Options A and B include NJ state certification sequences; Option C is purely academic/professional development.
Source: gse.rutgers.edu/degree/ed-m-in-educational-administration-and-supervision-option-c/; catalogs.rutgers.edu/generated/gse_2425/pg147.html

edm_educational_admin_option_a.major_name: Educational Administration and Supervision (EdM) — Option A (Principal + Supervisor Certification)
edm_educational_admin_option_a.description: Leads to NJ Principal and Supervisor certifications in addition to the EdM degree. Includes administrative internships in school settings.

required_courses.description: Required core and certification courses (24 credits)

constraints: Total: 36 credits.
Must hold valid NJ teaching certificate to pursue principal certification.
Internship placements (EADM530, EADM531) require school-based placement.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
notes: Option B leads only to Principal certification (no supervisor); requires fewer credits.
Source: gse.rutgers.edu/degree/ed-m-in-educational-administration-and-supervision-with-principal-and-supervisor-certification-option-a/

edm_education_culture_society.description: Interdisciplinary program examining education through historical, philosophical, sociological, anthropological, and policy lenses. Prepares students for careers in research, policy, advocacy, and community organizations.

electives.description: Elective courses from Social and Philosophical Foundations and related departments (21 credits)

capstone.description: Capstone: thesis or approved alternative (3 credits included in total)
capstone.notes: Students may write a thesis or complete a substantial paper demonstrating original inquiry.

constraints: Total: 30 credits.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
Program plan developed in consultation with faculty advisor.
notes: Source: gse.rutgers.edu/degree/ed-m-in-education-culture-and-society/; catalogs.rutgers.edu/generated/gse_2425/pg146.html

edm_learning_cognition_development.description: Provides a strong foundation in psychological research on learning, cognition, and human development. Prepares students for doctoral study or professional roles in education, research, human services, or the learning sciences.

core_coursework.description: One course from each of the three core areas (9 credits total)

outside_electives.description: Electives outside LCD core areas (6 credits)
outside_electives.notes: Cannot use courses with subject code 290 (Educational Psychology) or 295 (LCD) for this requirement. Advisor-approved from other GSE or graduate departments.

capstone.description: Capstone requirement (3 credits — included in total)

options.description: Research in LCD — recommended for doctoral-track students
Third LCD elective + 15-page term paper earning B+ or higher

constraints: Total: 30 credits minimum.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
ESME531 and ESME532 strongly recommended for students planning doctoral study.
Capstone option must be chosen in consultation with faculty advisor.
notes: Source: gse.rutgers.edu/degree/ed-m-in-learning-cognition-and-development/; catalogs.rutgers.edu/generated/gse_2425/pg140.html

edm_counseling_psychology.description: Prepares students for graduate-level counseling roles and provides the foundation for doctoral study in counseling psychology or clinical psychology. Note: this program does not lead directly to NJ licensure as a Licensed Professional Counselor (LPC); a separate supervised experience requirement applies post-degree.

required_core.description: Required counseling psychology core courses (30 credits)

practicum.notes: Three semesters of supervised counseling practicum required.

electives.description: Elective courses approved by advisor (6 credits)

constraints: Total: 48 credits.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
Competitive admissions; GRE scores and statement of purpose required.
Practicum placement arranged through GSE field experiences office.
This EdM alone does not satisfy NJ LPC licensure requirements.
notes: Program prepares students for doctoral applications in counseling and clinical psychology.
Source: gse.rutgers.edu/degree/ed-m-in-counseling-psychology/; catalogs.rutgers.edu/generated/gse_2425/pg136.html

edm_school_counseling.description: Prepares students for NJ School Counselor certification. Combines counseling theory, multicultural competency, school-based skills, and two supervised practicum experiences in school settings.

practicum.notes: Both practicum and internship must be completed in NJ-approved school settings.

constraints: Total: 48 credits.
NJ School Counselor certification requires passing Praxis II school counseling exam.
Must complete 600 supervised field hours across practicum and internship.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
CPSY627 requires concurrent enrollment with CPSY501 and CPSY502.
notes: Graduates are eligible to apply for NJ School Counselor certification upon completion.
Source: gse.rutgers.edu/degree/ed-m-with-certification-in-school-counseling/; catalogs.rutgers.edu/generated/gse_2425/

edm_special_education_general.description: Non-certification track for students seeking advanced study in special education without pursuing NJ Teacher of Students with Disabilities (TSD) certification. Appropriate for related service providers, administrators, and researchers.

electives.description: Elective courses from special education or related fields (15 credits)

constraints: Total: 33 credits.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
No certification internship required for General Studies track.
notes: Certification tracks (TSD, LDTC, ABA) require additional practicum and state licensing requirements.
Source: gse.rutgers.edu/degree/ed-m-in-special-education-general-studies/; catalogs.rutgers.edu/generated/gse_2425/

edm_special_education_tsd.major_name: Special Education with Teacher of Students with Disabilities Certification (EdM)
edm_special_education_tsd.description: Leads to NJ Teacher of Students with Disabilities (TSD) certification. Includes coursework in assessment, instruction, classroom management, and supervised practicum in inclusive and special education settings.

required_core.description: Required core courses for TSD certification (27 credits)

practicum.description: Supervised practicum in special education settings (6 credits)
practicum.notes: Practicum requires placement in both inclusive and self-contained special education classrooms.

constraints: Total: 36 credits.
TSD certification requires passing NJ Praxis II special education exam.
Must hold valid NJ teaching certificate or complete alternative route requirements.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
Practicum must be completed in NJ-approved schools.
notes: Additional TSD tracks with ABA sequence or Gifted Ed certificate also available.
Source: gse.rutgers.edu/degree/ed-m-in-special-education-with-teacher-of-students-with-disabilities-certification/; catalogs.rutgers.edu/generated/gse_2425/

edm_literacy_education.description: Prepares educators to become expert literacy teachers and leaders across grades PK–12. Develops theoretical foundations in reading/writing, diagnostic skills, and curriculum design for diverse learners.

electives.description: Elective courses from reading, language, or related fields (15 credits)

constraints: Total: 33 credits.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
READ565 (clinical practicum) must be completed after READ564.
notes: May be combined with Reading Specialist endorsement program (additional credits/internship required).

edm_mathematics_education.description: Deepens mathematical and pedagogical knowledge for practicing or aspiring mathematics teachers at the elementary, middle, or secondary level. Can be completed without a teaching certification.

required_core.description: Required core mathematics education courses (18 credits)

electives.description: Elective courses from mathematics education or related GSE fields (12 credits)

constraints: Total: 30 credits.

notes: Initial K-12 certification variant (with student teaching) also available as a separate track.

edm_science_education.description: Designed for practicing science educators and those seeking initial certification in biological or physical science. Provides research-based pedagogy, curriculum analysis, and inquiry-based teaching methods.

required_core.description: Required core science education courses (15 credits)

electives.description: Elective courses from science education or related fields (15 credits)

constraints: Total: 30 credits.

edm_social_studies_education.description: Designed for practicing social studies educators and those seeking initial K-12 certification. Focuses on curriculum analysis, multicultural perspectives, civic education, and inquiry-based teaching.

edm_educational_statistics.description: Prepares applied statisticians, psychometricians, and evaluation specialists for roles in education, government, and research organizations. Strong quantitative and technical focus.

electives.description: Elective courses from statistics, measurement, or related fields (12 credits)

constraints: Total: 30 credits.
Minimum GPA of 3.0 required.
Undergraduate statistics and mathematics background expected.
Program plan developed in consultation with faculty advisor.
notes: Prepares students for roles as data analysts, evaluators, and psychometricians.
Source: gse.rutgers.edu/degree/ed-m-in-educational-statistics-measurement-evaluation/; catalogs.rutgers.edu/generated/gse_2425/

edm_language_education.description: Prepares language educators and researchers in areas including ESL, bilingual/bicultural education, world language education, and applied linguistics. Multiple endorsement and certification tracks available.

required_core.description: Required core language education courses (15 credits)

electives.description: Elective courses from language, literacy, or related fields (15 credits)

endorsement_options.description: Optional endorsement tracks that require additional fieldwork courses
options: ESL (English as a Second Language) Endorsement — add LANE539 + CPSY507 + field experience
Bilingual/Bicultural Endorsement — add LANE519, LANE522 + field experience
ESL + Bilingual/Bicultural — combined track with additional credits

notes: One of the most versatile GSE programs; serves ESL, world language, and bilingual educators.
